ddls:
CREATE TABLE "passenger_data" (
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger */,
  "survived" INTEGER /* Survival status (1 = survived, 0 = not survived) */,
  "pclass" INTEGER /* Passenger class (1st, 2nd, or 3rd class) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the passenger */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the passenger (male or female) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the passenger */,
  "sibsp" INTEGER /* Number of siblings/spouses aboard */,
  "parch" INTEGER /* Number of parents/children aboard */,
  "ticket" TEXT /* Ticket number */,
  "fare" REAL /* Fare paid by the passenger */,
  "cabin" TEXT /* Cabin number */,
  "embarked" TEXT /* Port of embarkation (C = Cherbourg; Q = Queenstown; S = Southampton) */,
  "age_random" INTEGER /* Randomly generated age for anonymity purposes */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Passenger nationality */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of emergency contact */,
  "travel_companion_id" INTEGER /* ID of travel companion (if any) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("passenger_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "voyage_data" (
  "voyage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each voyage */,
  "ship_name" TEXT /* Name of the ship */,
  "voyage_date" TEXT /* Date of the voyage */,
  "route" TEXT /* Route taken by the ship */,
  "captain_name" TEXT /* Name of the captain */,
  "crew_count" INTEGER /* Number of crew members */,
  "passenger_count" INTEGER /* Number of passengers */,
  "disaster_status" INTEGER /* Was the voyage a disaster (1 = yes, 0 = no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("voyage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ship_data" (
  "ship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ship */,
  "ship_name" TEXT /* Name of the ship */,
  "ship_type" TEXT /* Type of ship (e.g., passenger liner, cargo ship) */,
  "length" REAL /* Length of the ship (in meters) */,
  "beam" REAL /* Beam of the ship (in meters) */,
  "gross_tonnage" INTEGER /* Gross tonnage of the ship */,
  "builder" TEXT /* Shipbuilder */,
  "year_built" INTEGER /* Year the ship was built */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_data" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, actuary, researcher) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_log" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, data access) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "family_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family relationship */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger */,
  "relative_id" INTEGER /* ID of the relative */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., sibling, parent, child) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "voyage_passengers" (
  "voyage_passenger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each voyage-passenger entry */,
  "voyage_id" INTEGER /* ID of the voyage */,
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* ID of the passenger */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("voyage_passenger_id")
)
column_names: Sex of the passenger (male or female)
Port of embarkation (C = Cherbourg; Q = Queenstown; S = Southampton)
Randomly generated age for anonymity purposes
Was the voyage a disaster (1 = yes, 0 = no)
Type of ship (e.g., passenger liner, cargo ship)
Role of the user (e.g., analyst, actuary, researcher)
Unique identifier for each access log entry
